commits.note: GREEN was amended once to fold in an error-message accuracy refinement (engine-only string, asserted by no test); the TS fresh-clone gate was re-run at the final SHA after the amend.

F1_temporal_round_trip.class: TC-47 — calendar-impossible DAY rollover residue behind the TC-44 strftime-leniency class
F1_temporal_round_trip.bug: strftime('%s', ?) ROLLS OVER a shape-valid but impossible DAY instead of returning NULL: 2025-02-30T00:00:00Z -> epoch for 2025-03-02; 2025-04-31T00:00:00Z -> 2025-05-01. TC-44's is_iso8601_shape gate checks FORMAT not calendar validity, so Feb-30 passed the gate and stored a DIFFERENT instant than the provider supplied — bypassing the ratified hard-reject contract. (Impossible MONTHS like 2025-13-01 and impossible TIMES like 25:00:00 / :60 / :61 already NULL out; only impossible DAYS roll over — the sole residue.)
F1_temporal_round_trip.fix_location: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — iso8601_to_epoch_seconds (the shared parse used by normalize_extractor_timestamp for both t_valid and t_invalid at the fathomdb.extract.v1 write boundary).
F1_temporal_round_trip.round_trip_sql: SELECT CAST(strftime('%s', ?1) AS INTEGER) WHERE strftime('%Y-%m-%d', substr(?1, 1, 10)) IS substr(?1, 1, 10)
F1_temporal_round_trip.sql_rationale: The WHERE clause is the round-trip: the literal calendar DATE component of the input (substr(?1,1,10) = the YYYY-MM-DD the shape gate guarantees present) must survive SQLite's own calendar math UNCHANGED. On a DAY rollover, strftime('%Y-%m-%d', substr) differs from the literal substring, the WHERE yields zero rows, query_row -> QueryReturnedNoRows -> .ok().flatten() -> None, and the EXISTING hard-reject Err path in normalize_extractor_timestamp fires. The shape gate is KEPT for its early clear message; the round-trip is the completeness backstop behind it (defence in depth).
F1_temporal_round_trip.why_date_component_not_raw_or_utc_render: A raw-string comparison or a strftime(...,'unixepoch') UTC re-render would FALSE-REJECT valid equivalent forms, because the UTC render shifts the wall clock for a non-UTC offset and its date can differ from the input's literal date. Comparing ONLY the literal DATE field (substr 1..10) is timezone-INVARIANT — the offset never alters the input's own YYYY-MM-DD text — while still catching every DAY rollover, because the rollover happens in the calendar math BEFORE the offset is applied. This is the tz trap the mandate warned about, engineered out.
F1_temporal_round_trip.completeness_superset: The round-trip is a SUPERSET of the shape gate for the Julian case: substr('2451545.0',1,10) = '2451545.0' renders via strftime('%Y-%m-%d',...) to '2000-01-01', which != '2451545.0' -> rejected. So the TC-44 Julian string is rejected by the round-trip independently of the shape gate.
F1_temporal_round_trip.no_date_crate: CONFIRMED — pure SQL. No chrono/time (or any date crate) added; the workspace still has none. That option was OFF the table per mandate.
F1_temporal_round_trip.null_reject_path_untouched: CONFIRMED — the None/JSON-null/absent -> Ok(None) arm of normalize_extractor_timestamp (NULL means 'still valid') is byte-unchanged. The fix only widened the set of String inputs that iso8601_to_epoch_seconds returns None for (rolled-over DAYS), which routes into the EXISTING hard-reject Err path — never into the NULL/Ok(None) path. No resurrection-via-NULL surface was opened.
F1_temporal_round_trip.surviving_class_escalation: NONE. No malformed-but-well-shaped temporal value survives the round-trip: impossible MONTHS and impossible TIMES already NULL out via strftime; impossible DAYS are caught by the DATE round-trip; the Julian string is caught by both the shape gate and the round-trip. No fix-N past the round-trip was added; no escalation to 'add a date library' was triggered.

positive_forms_accepted_not_false_reject.proof: All five ACCEPTED and stored at the CORRECT epoch, asserted at the raw at-rest edge table (t_valid, typeof=integer) in tc47_timestamp_round_trip.rs. The +05:00 case (1742445000) is the load-bearing tz-trap control: its epoch differs from the +00:00/Z case (1742463000) by exactly 5h, proving the offset is honoured and not false-rejected.

F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.class: AC-068 / TC-47 — TS configureProjections skipped the JS-side FFI surrogate guard
F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.bug: src/ts/src/index.ts configureProjections did NOT call the FFI string guard before the native call. napi-rs silently replaces a lone UTF-16 surrogate with U+FFFD BEFORE the Rust-side guard runs, so a JS caller passing a name/role/ftsTokenizer/vectorEmbedder/drop entry with a lone surrogate got U+FFFD persisted instead of WriteValidationError. (A NUL survives the napi UTF-8 path as a real byte and was already caught Rust-side — which is why the pre-existing NUL tests passed but the surrogate leaked.)
F2_ts_projection_ffi_guard.fix_location: src/ts/src/index.ts — configureProjections now calls validateFfiTree(specs) and validateFfiTree(drop) BEFORE this.#native.configureProjections(...). validateFfiTree recursively validates every string in the tree: projection name, each role, ftsTokenizer, vectorEmbedder, and each drop entry. Mirrors the existing write() call site (validateFfiTree(batch)) and the AC-068b surrogate handling in validation.ts.

F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.class: [P2] a filter on an UNDECLARED attribute emits raw SQL -> `no such column` crash; text arm diverges (silent no-match). ADR-0.8.11 D3 total-dispatch violation.
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.bug: vector_filter_clause lowered every SearchFilter.attributes term to `AND attr_<hex>=?` in the vec0 phase-1 KNN. For a name that is NOT a declared `filterable` projection there is no such vec0 column -> SQLite `no such column` -> the reader worker Err propagated as an opaque EngineError::Storage on the vector arm; meanwhile the FTS arm's hit_attributes_pass_filter probed canonical_attributes, found no row, and silently NO-MATCHED. A caller typo (or a filter issued before configure_projections) therefore became a divergent storage/search FAILURE instead of a stable, arm-uniform typed outcome.
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.fix_location: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — NEW fn validate_filter_attributes_declared(conn, filter) called ONCE in Engine::search_inner_with_stats, after the empty-query check and BEFORE the reader dispatch (so it precedes BOTH arms). Validates each attribute name against the declared `filterable` registry set (load_projection_registry -> ProjectionRole::Filterable membership).
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.typed_rejection_mechanism: Reuses the EXISTING EngineError::InvalidFilter { reason } variant (the same variant D3 uses to typed-reject an unsupported json-path predicate on search_filtered). reason names the attribute: `filter attribute "nonexistent" is not a declared `filterable` projection; declare it via configure_projections before filtering on it`. NO new error variant.
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.both_arms_agree_proof: The validation runs before ReaderPool dispatch, so NEITHER arm executes on an undeclared name -> the fused two-arm search_filtered (which exercises BOTH the vector and FTS arms) returns ONE consistent InvalidFilter. Pinned by undeclared_filter_attribute_is_typed_rejection_on_both_arms (slice15e_prekn_filterable.rs): assertion (1) matches Err(InvalidFilter{reason}) with reason.contains("nonexistent"); pre-fix this arm got EngineError::Storage (the raw `no such column`).
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.why_registry_set_not_actual_columns: The registry `filterable` set is the arm-INDEPENDENT authority: it is correct even with no embedder / no vector_default table, where a declared-`filterable` term still filters legitimately via the row-owned canonical_attributes EAV store (the FTS arm). Gating solely on actual_vector_attr_columns would over-reject a valid declared-filterable text-arm filter when no embedder is configured. reconcile_vector_attr_columns keeps the live vec0 shape == desired_vector_attr_columns (== the registry filterable set) on every configure_projections AND on boot (open-time reconcile at lib.rs ~4459), so a registry-`filterable` name always has its vec0 column when the vector arm runs.
F1_undeclared_filter_attribute.controls: Control (2): a DECLARED attribute with an ABSENT value still NO-MATCHES (Ok result excluding the rows) — fix-3 behaviour UNCHANGED, NOT a rejection. Control (3): the declared present value still MATCHES (non-vacuous — the fix did not turn every attribute filter into a rejection). Both green pre- and post-fix.

F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.class: [P2] a deferred-only registry mutation is reported as `unchanged` — an accepted mutation lies to SDK callers as an idempotent no-op.
F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.bug: apply_projection_config's Some(existing)-changed (non-destructive persist) branch gated the delta.deferred push on `desired.has_deferred() && !existing.has_deferred()`. For a change that STAYED deferred (rankable -> rankable+vector: vector_declared false->true, adds a deferred vector sub-target, builds no EAV) the row WAS persisted but !existing.has_deferred() was false AND wants_eav() was false, so built/deferred/dropped were all empty -> delta.unchanged computed true.
F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.fix_location: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs (~16148) — dropped the `&& !existing.has_deferred()` guard; the changed branch now pushes delta.deferred `if desired.has_deferred()`, mirroring the fresh-registration (None) branch.
F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.completeness_argument: Every valid non-empty spec has wants_eav() (filterable||searchable) OR has_deferred() (rankable||vector_declared) — a spec with none has empty roles and is rejected up front. The changed branch runs ONLY when existing != desired. So on a real change at least one of built/deferred is now populated => delta.unchanged can never be true on a persisted change. No sibling remains.
F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.no_op_preserved_proof: Re-registering the IDENTICAL spec takes the `Some(existing) if existing == &desired` idempotent arm (untouched) => empty delta, unchanged=true. Pinned by deferred_only_mutation_is_reported_not_unchanged control (third apply: unchanged=true, empty delta) AND the pre-existing idempotent_reregistration_is_a_noop / idempotent_reregistration_holds_for_deferred_rankable / idempotent_refilterable_registration_is_vec0_noop tests (all still green).
F2_deferred_only_mutation_reported_uncha.registry_delta_alignment: The F2 test asserts read_projections() after the accepted mutation reflects the new vector sub-target (read[0].vector.is_some()) AND delta.deferred names it — exactly what the delta claimed.

self_audit.drop_that_changes_deferred_set: ALREADY-CORRECT — a drop reports delta.dropped; REDUCING a deferred target (removing the rankable role or the vector sub-object) is is_destructive_projection_change => ProjectionDestructive error requiring an explicit drop => the drop+rebuild is reported in dropped + built/deferred. destructive_change_requires_explicit_drop and name_in_both_specs_and_drop_is_the_supported_rebuild stay green.

So the F2 completeness argument (built OR deferred always populated on a non-destructive change) has no residue.
self_audit.sibling_other_search_entry_points: COVERED — every filtered-search entry point (search, search_view, search_reranked[_view], search_explained, search_filter, search_filtered) funnels through search_reranked_with_explain -> search_inner -> search_inner_with_stats; search_expand calls search_inner directly. The single validation point in search_inner_with_stats therefore guards ALL of them. No read.list/read.collection sibling: those use the unified read Filter path and do not emit attr_<hex> vec0 predicates (out of the mandated projection/filter surface anyway).

fix_3.scope: 0.8.20 keystone closeout fix-3 — the ONE non-temporal codex §9 [P2] TOCTOU race between attribute-filter validation and the reader snapshot. ADD-only on branch orch-0.8.20-s15b; fix-1 (temporal) and fix-2 (undeclared-attr + deferred delta) are untouched.
fix_3.baseline_head: 37dd4f67102a8babdc7fc8195f30ca04ce4ac254 (fix-2 witness — head at which fix-3 was commissioned)
fix_3.status: COMPLETE — the race is closed via a snapshot-scoping change (validate INSIDE the reader's deferred transaction). NOT an architectural rewrite. TDD RED->GREEN; all gates green.

finding.class: [P2] TOCTOU — fix-2's undeclared-`filterable`-attribute validation (validate_filter_attributes_declared) ran in search_inner_with_stats on the WRITER connection (self.connection.lock()) and RELEASED that lock BEFORE the reader worker prepared+executed the vec0 phase-1 KNN on its OWN pooled connection/snapshot.
finding.bug: A configure_projections DROP of the filtered `filterable` projection, committed in the window between the writer-connection validation (which passed: attr still declared) and the moment the reader pinned its DEFERRED-tx snapshot, removed the attr_<hex> vec0 column. vector_filter_clause still emitted `AND attr_<hex>=?` for that now-absent column => SQLite `no such column` => the reader worker returned rusqlite::Error => search_inner_with_stats collapsed EVERY reader Err to EngineError::Storage — the exact opaque/untyped failure fix-2 was written to prevent, re-introduced by concurrency. Two connections => two potentially-divergent schema snapshots => the check and the query could disagree.
finding.the_exact_txn_connection_change: Validation MOVED from a pre-dispatch check on self.connection (the writer) INTO read_search_in_tx, the reader worker function that opens the DEFERRED read transaction and prepares+executes BOTH the vec0 phase-1 SQL and the FTS arm. NEW fn validate_filter_attributes_on_snapshot(&Connection, &SearchFilter) -> Result<(), SearchReaderError> is called immediately AFTER `let tx = reader.transaction_with_behavior(Deferred)?` and the snapshot-pinning `load_projection_cursor(&tx)?`, and BEFORE build_vector_phase1_sql (vector arm) and hit_attributes_pass_filter (FTS arm). It reads the projection registry from `&tx` — the SAME deferred transaction the vec0 query runs in — so SQLite WAL read-snapshot isolation guarantees the `_fathomdb_projection_registry` rows it checks and the `vector_default` columns the query compiles against are ONE consistent schema snapshot. The pre-dispatch writer-connection check was REMOVED (single snapshot authority).
finding.snapshot_consistency_argument: read_search_in_tx opens BEGIN DEFERRED (query_only reader); the snapshot pins on the first read (load_projection_cursor) and is frozen for the life of the tx. A concurrent configure_projections DROP on the writer connection either (a) commits BEFORE the snapshot pins => the reader's snapshot INCLUDES it => the in-tx registry read shows the attr no longer `filterable` AND the vec0 column is absent => validate_filter_attributes_on_snapshot returns InvalidFilter (consistent typed outcome); or (b) commits AFTER the snapshot pins => it is INVISIBLE to this tx => the registry still shows the attr declared AND the vec0 column still exists in that snapshot => the query runs against exactly the column it validated. There is no third case: the registry membership and the vec0 column existence can never disagree WITHIN one snapshot, so the `no such column` path is unreachable. The window fix-2 left open (validate on connection X, execute on connection Y) is gone because X == Y == the one reader tx.
finding.both_arms_share_the_snapshot_proof: validate_filter_attributes_on_snapshot runs ONCE at the top of read_search_in_tx, before EITHER arm: the vector arm (build_vector_phase1_sql / vector_filter_clause emitting attr_<hex>=?) and the FTS arm (hit_attributes_pass_filter probing canonical_attributes) both execute later in the SAME `tx`. So both arms are gated by the identical registry snapshot the vec0 query compiles against — a concurrent drop yields ONE consistent InvalidFilter across both arms, never a vector-arm Storage crash diverging from an FTS-arm silent no-match. search_text_only_view passes filter=None, so it carries no attribute surface (nothing to validate/diverge).
finding.typed_rejection_mechanism: NO new error variant. A new INTERNAL reader-channel error enum SearchReaderError { Sqlite(rusqlite::Error), InvalidFilter(String) } replaces the bare rusqlite::Error in the Search-path ReaderResponse alias (used ONLY by read_search_in_tx + its two caller sites; From<rusqlite::Error> keeps every existing `?` working). The two Search callers map SearchReaderError::InvalidFilter(reason) -> the EXISTING EngineError::InvalidFilter { reason } (already mapped by Py fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs -> InvalidFilterError and TS src/ts/src/errors.ts -> InvalidFilterError) and SearchReaderError::Sqlite -> emit_sqlite_internal_error + EngineError::Storage (prior behaviour verbatim).
finding.not_a_regression_of_fix2_or_fix3: fix-2 undeclared-attr rejection: undeclared_filter_attribute_is_typed_rejection_on_both_arms STILL PASSES — the non-concurrent undeclared name is now caught by the reader-snapshot check (which still runs before either arm) instead of the removed pre-dispatch check; the typed InvalidFilter reason string is byte-identical, so reason.contains("nonexistent") holds. fix-3 declared-but-absent no-match + declared-present match controls (2) and (3) UNCHANGED and green. Removing the pre-dispatch writer check also eliminates a per-filtered-search writer-lock acquisition and fix-2's concurrent-ADD false-reject (the reader snapshot now sees a freshly-added declaration and accepts).
finding.test_seam: reader_search_hook (module + arm_reader_search_hook_for_test / clear_reader_search_hook_for_test, `#[doc(hidden)]`, OFF the governed surface, mirroring set_vector_stage_only_for_test). Fired at the TOP of read_search_in_tx, BEFORE the deferred snapshot pins, so a test can commit a concurrent DROP in the exact race window and make the TOCTOU deterministic. Disarmed by default: one Relaxed atomic load per search (same class as the four hot-path atomics already read there), fires at most once (the closure is take()n), no-op in production.

red_evidence.result: test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 10 filtered out; exit 101
red_evidence.panic: panicked at slice15e_prekn_filterable.rs:1037:38 — 'TOCTOU: a configure_projections DROP racing the search made the vec0 attr_<hex> column vanish AFTER the pre-dispatch check passed on the writer connection, so the reader crashed with an opaque `no such column` Storage error. fix-3 must validate on the reader's OWN transaction snapshot so this is a typed InvalidFilter.'
red_evidence.what_it_asserts_and_why_it_captures_the_: The RED harness (SearchReaderError plumbing + reader_search_hook, but NO reader-side validation yet) parks the reader worker at the hook, DROPs `priority` on the writer connection in the exact validate->execute window, releases the reader, then asserts a typed EngineError::InvalidFilter. The pre-dispatch writer check ALREADY PASSED (priority declared when the search thread validated), and the reader then pins a post-drop snapshot where attr_<hex> is gone => today it hits Err(EngineError::Storage) (the panic arm). This is the strongest RED the mandate describes: a deterministic interleave (validate -> concurrent drop -> execute) that today yields Storage and after the fix yields InvalidFilter — the two connections are proven divergent.

bindings.decision: SKIPPED fresh-clone X1 + pyright + TS suite per mandate — NO new error variant and NO binding/stub touched. The fix reuses EngineError::InvalidFilter (already mapped by BOTH bindings: fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs -> InvalidFilterError; src/ts/src/errors.ts -> InvalidFilterError). SearchReaderError is a crate-INTERNAL enum on the reader channel; it never crosses the FFI boundary. No Python/TS source changed.

invariants.AC-041: GREEN — no recovery/verb code touched; recovery_denylist stays five {recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild}. Diff confined to the reader-path validation move + the SearchReaderError channel enum + the reader_search_hook test seam + the one new test.
invariants.governed_surface: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted, never HITL-SIGNED.
invariants.no_new_error_variant: CONFIRMED — reused EngineError::InvalidFilter; SearchReaderError is an internal reader-channel enum, not a public/binding surface.
invariants.no_architectural_escalation: The race was closed by 'validate within the reader snapshot / hold one read transaction across validate+execute' exactly as the mandate scoped — NOT a larger rewrite. No STOP-and-report trigger fired.

ReaderResponse alias now carries SearchReaderError (new internal enum + From<rusqlite::Error>); reader_search_hook module + arm/clear _for_test seams; read_search_in_tx fires the hook (pre-snapshot) then calls validate_filter_attributes_on_snapshot inside its deferred tx; validate_filter_attributes_declared (writer-conn, EngineError) replaced by validate_filter_attributes_on_snapshot (reader-conn, SearchReaderError); pre-dispatch validation block removed from search_inner_with_stats; both Search caller sites destructure SearchReaderError (InvalidFilter -> EngineError::InvalidFilter, Sqlite -> Storage).
